mapQuestions.question: Which continent is Sri Lanka on?
mapQuestions.answer: Asia. It is a teardrop-shaped island just south of India 🌏

mapQuestions.question: What is the capital of Sri Lanka?
mapQuestions.answer: Colombo is the biggest city and commercial capital, but the official political capital is a town next door called Sri Jayawardenepura Kotte. Most people just say Colombo.

mapQuestions.question: What ocean surrounds Sri Lanka?
mapQuestions.answer: The Indian Ocean. The country has 1,340km of coastline, almost all of it beach.

mapQuestions.question: What is Sri Lanka most famous for growing?
mapQuestions.answer: Tea. The British turned huge parts of the hill country into tea plantations in the 1800s, and "Ceylon tea" (Sri Lanka's old name) is still one of the best in the world.

mapQuestions.question: What languages do Sri Lankans speak?
mapQuestions.answer: Sinhala and Tamil are both official languages. About 75% of people speak Sinhala (mostly Buddhists), and 25% speak Tamil (mostly Hindu in the north and east).

foods.name: Hoppers
foods.description: Bowl-shaped pancakes made from fermented rice flour and coconut milk, with a crispy edge and soft middle. An "egg hopper" has an egg cracked into the middle as it cooks. Eaten for breakfast or dinner.

foods.name: Kottu roti
foods.description: Chopped flatbread stir-fried on a hot griddle with vegetables, egg, meat and curry sauce. The chopping is done with two metal blades that clang against the griddle, kottu KOTTU kottu KOTTU.

foods.name: Rice and curry
foods.description: The Sri Lankan main meal: a mountain of rice surrounded by 5-7 small bowls of different curries (dhal, jackfruit, beetroot, beans, pumpkin, fish), plus sambols and a poppadom. You mix it all up with your right hand.

foods.name: Pol sambol
foods.description: Freshly grated coconut, chilli, lime, red onion and dried fish, mixed together until fiery red. The most famous Sri Lankan condiment, eaten with everything from rice to bread.

foods.name: String hoppers
foods.description: Nests of steamed rice-flour noodles, light as feathers, eaten dipped in coconut sambol and a fish or dhal curry. A classic Sri Lankan breakfast.

foods.name: Watalappan
foods.description: A rich coconut and palm sugar (jaggery) custard, spiced with cardamom and topped with cashews. The most popular Sri Lankan dessert, especially among the Muslim community.

scavengerItems.label: A tuk-tuk with a slogan painted on the back ("Slow but Steady", "Born to be Wild", etc.)
A field of tea bushes stretching to the horizon
A monkey on a roof, a wire, or near a temple
Someone hacking open a king coconut (orange-coloured) with a machete
A working elephant or a wild elephant in a national park
A blue train hugging the southern coast or winding through tea plantations
A fisherman on a stilt out at sea, balanced above the waves
A small Buddha statue beside the road, sometimes inside a glass case
A water buffalo standing in a paddy field at sunrise
A jeweller's shop in Ratnapura advertising sapphires
A Hindu temple (kovil) with brightly painted statues stacked into a tower
A sign in BOTH Sinhala and Tamil scripts (look how different they are)

geoMatches.description: The biggest city, on the west coast. A mix of old colonial buildings, modern skyscrapers, busy markets, and a long oceanfront promenade where families gather at sunset.
The cultural capital in the hill country. Home of the Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic (said to hold one of Buddha's teeth) and the country's most important Buddhist site.
An old port town on the south coast, with a Dutch fort that wraps around it. Cobbled streets, cafés, and high stone walls that protected the town from the 2004 tsunami.
A 200-metre stone column rising straight out of the jungle in the centre of the country. On top sits the ruins of a 5th-century palace.
A small town high in the hill country, surrounded by tea plantations. The blue train journey from Kandy to Ella is one of the most beautiful in the world.
A national park in the south-east. Has one of the highest density of leopards in the world, plus elephants, sloth bears, peacocks and crocodiles.

senses.smell: Coconut and cinnamon in every dish, cardamom-spiced tea, frangipani at temples, the Indian Ocean, woodsmoke from village stoves, fish drying in the sun…
senses.hear: The "kottu kottu" of metal blades chopping roti, monsoon rain on tin roofs, temple drums at sunset, Buddhist chanting from radios, "Ayubowan!", monkeys in the jungle…
senses.taste: Fiery coconut sambol, sweet hot Ceylon tea, creamy coconut curries, the punch of fresh chilli, sour lime, salty fish, sticky jaggery sweets…
senses.feel: Sticky tropical heat near the coast, cool mountain air in the hill country, the wobble of a tuk-tuk, warm ocean waves, the breeze of a train with the doors wide open…
senses.see: What is the most incredible thing right in front of you right now…

stories.question: Who built a palace on top of a 200m rock?
stories.body: In the middle of the Sri Lankan jungle, a single enormous lump of granite rises 200 metres straight up out of the trees. On top, 1,500 years ago, King Kashyapa built himself a royal palace. He had killed his father to take the throne, and was hiding from his half-brother who wanted revenge, so he chose the safest possible spot. To get to the palace you had to climb past frescoes of beautiful women painted onto the rock face (around 500 of them, only a few survive today), and through the mouth of a huge stone lion (only its giant paws remain). The half-brother eventually arrived with an army. Kashyapa came down from the rock to fight, lost, and killed himself. The palace was abandoned and forgotten in the jungle for centuries.
stories.thinkingQuestion: If you had to build a hidden home on top of a giant rock, what would you absolutely need up there with you?

stories.question: What is the most beautiful train ride in the world?
stories.body: The blue train from Kandy to Ella climbs through the hill country of Sri Lanka over about 7 hours. It snakes through emerald tea plantations, over high arched bridges, into long tunnels, and past tiny stations with names painted on wooden boards. The doors stay wide open the whole time, and you can sit on the steps with your legs dangling out as the train rocks gently along the cliffs. People sell tea, samosas and biscuits walking up and down the aisles. The train is over 100 years old, built by the British when they ruled Sri Lanka, and barely anything has changed. There are no air-conditioned tourist trains, this is the same train Sri Lankans take to work or school.
stories.thinkingQuestion: If you could design a 7-hour train journey through anywhere in the world, where would the train go?

stories.question: Why are leopards so easy to spot in one Sri Lankan park?
stories.body: Yala in the south-east of Sri Lanka has one of the highest densities of leopards in the world, around one per square kilometre. They are easier to see here than anywhere else on earth, because Sri Lankan leopards are the top predator on this island. They do not have to fear lions or tigers (there are none), so they walk around in the open during the day. You also share the park with hundreds of elephants, water buffalo, sloth bears, crocodiles, mongooses and peacocks. Tourists travel through in open jeeps, and a single morning drive can have you spotting more wildlife than a year in the British countryside.

stories.question: What was the Boxing Day tsunami?
stories.body: On the morning of 26 December 2004, a huge earthquake (magnitude 9.1) hit deep under the sea off Sumatra in Indonesia. It triggered a tsunami, a series of giant waves, that raced across the Indian Ocean at the speed of a jumbo jet. About two hours later, walls of water 9 metres high crashed onto the entire eastern and southern coasts of Sri Lanka. Around 35,000 Sri Lankans died, and over a million were left homeless. There were no warning systems back then. The Hikkaduwa fort in Galle famously stopped the waves, saving the town inside. Today there are tsunami warning towers and sirens along the coast, and December 26 is a national day of remembrance.
stories.thinkingQuestion: After a disaster, how do you think a place finds the strength to rebuild?

stories.question: Why is Sri Lanka famous for its blue stones?
stories.body: Sri Lanka has been mining gemstones for over 2,000 years. The town of Ratnapura (which literally means "City of Gems") sits on top of riverbeds packed with sapphires, rubies, garnets, topaz and cat's-eyes. The most famous Sri Lankan stone is the blue sapphire, a deep cornflower blue. The huge sapphire in the engagement ring Prince William gave to Kate Middleton (which was first given by Charles to Diana) is a Sri Lankan stone. Most mining is still done by hand, with men chest-deep in muddy pits sieving gravel through wooden trays for hours. A good clear blue sapphire can pay for a house. Most fail to find one.

convoQuestions.question: What's the best thing you've eaten in Sri Lanka?
How is eating with your right hand? Easier or harder than chopsticks?
What is one thing Sri Lankans do better than at home?
Have you found your favourite Sri Lankan place yet? Why?
The blue train, the leopards, or the rock fortress, which would you most want to come back for?
Sri Lanka has had a long civil war and a tsunami in living memory, yet it is one of the most welcoming countries on earth. What does that tell you about people?

wordSearch.hint: Sri Lankan foods, places and things to spot.

animals.name: Sri Lankan elephant
animals.description: Around 6,000 still in the wild. Yala and Udawalawe parks are the best places.

animals.name: Toque macaque
animals.description: Cheeky monkeys on every temple wall, with a comical "haircut" of fur on the head.

animals.name: Sri Lankan leopard
animals.description: Yala National Park has one of the highest densities of leopards in the world.

animals.name: Indian peafowl
animals.description: Bright iridescent blue, displaying tail-fans on Sigiriya rock or in dry-zone gardens.

animals.name: Blue whale
animals.description: Off Mirissa and Trincomalee, you can see the biggest animal that has ever lived.

animals.name: Hawksbill turtle
animals.description: On the south and east coasts, especially around Rekawa and Kosgoda beaches.
